headlines.headline: Iran parliament to vote on €50 million bounty for Trump and Netanyahu
headlines.summary: Iran’s National Security Council chairman announced a bill offering rewards for killing US President Trump, Israeli PM Netanyahu, and CENTCOM commander Cooper, calling it “reciprocal action” for the assassination of former Supreme Leader Khamenei.

headlines.headline: Explosions heard on Qeshm Island in strategic Strait of Hormuz
headlines.summary: Explosions were reported on Qeshm Island between the Persian Gulf and Strait of Hormuz, with air defenses activated amid heightened tensions following US warnings of possible strikes.

headlines.headline: Japan and South Korea deepen energy cooperation amid Hormuz crisis
headlines.summary: Both nations, heavily dependent on Middle Eastern oil imports, agreed to joint oil storage arrangements as the Strait of Hormuz closure threatens energy supplies. Seoul set fuel price ceilings for the first time in three decades.

headlines.headline: IDF destroys 60% of Hezbollah infrastructure in southern Lebanon
headlines.summary: A senior IDF Northern Command officer said Israeli forces have destroyed 60% of Hezbollah terror infrastructure across 600 square kilometers in 63 villages within the Forward Defense Line zone.

headlines.headline: China joins global sell-off of US Treasuries as Iran war sparks panic
headlines.summary: China cut its US Treasury holdings by  billion in March, joining Japan and other major holders in reducing exposure amid inflation fears and energy uncertainties from the Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: NATO Jet Shoots Down Drone Over Estonia, Baltic Security Heightened
headlines.summary: A NATO fighter jet shot down a reported stray Ukrainian drone over Estonia, according to the Estonian defense minister. The incident highlights growing concerns about airspace security in the Baltic region amid the ongoing Russia-Ukraine conflict.

headlines.headline: Gunmen Abduct 39 Students and 7 Teachers in Nigeria School Attacks
headlines.summary: Armed gunmen kidnapped 39 students and 7 teachers in coordinated attacks targeting a secondary school and two primary schools in Nigeria. Children aged between two and 16 were among those abducted in the latest mass kidnapping.

headlines.headline: Sweden to Buy Navy Frigates From France for Over $4 Billion to Boost Baltic Defence
headlines.summary: Sweden will order four navy frigates from Frances Naval Group in a major deal that will triple its air defence capacity. The new NATO member focuses on security threats in the Baltic Sea amid heightened regional tensions.

headlines.headline: NATO Jet Shoots Down Suspected Drone in Estonia Amid Regional Tensions
headlines.summary: A NATO aircraft shot down a suspected drone in Estonia, the latest in a series of airspace violations in the region amid frequent Ukrainian attacks on Russia.

headlines.headline: Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Plant Reaching Point of No Return, Rosatom Head Warns
headlines.summary: The situation around the Russian-held Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant in Ukraine is reaching a critical point due to increased Ukrainian attacks in the area, according to the head of Russias state nuclear corporation.

perspectives.angle: Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Plant Reaching Point of No Return, Rosatom Warns
perspectives.summary: The head of Russia state nuclear corporation Rosatom warned that increased Ukrainian attacks near the Russian-held Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant are pushing the situation to a critical point, raising fears of a nuclear catastrophe in southeastern Ukraine.

headlines.headline: Polish PM Urges Transatlantic Unity After Pentagon Cancels Troop Deployment
headlines.summary: Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk stressed the importance of ties with Washington after the Pentagon cancelled plans to deploy 4,000 U.S.-based troops to Poland, calling for transatlantic unity to survive a difficult period.

perspectives.angle: Polish PM Urges Transatlantic Unity After US Troop Deployment Cancelled
perspectives.summary: Donald Tusk stressed the importance of Poland's ties to Washington after the Pentagon cancelled plans to deploy 4,000 US-based troops to Poland, calling for NATO unity during a difficult period.

headlines.headline: Chinese aircraft carrier conducts live-fire drills in Western Pacific
headlines.summary: The Liaoning carrier group is holding exercises months after a stand-off with Japanese fighter jets, highlighting rising military tensions in the region.

headlines.headline: Türkiye detains 110 suspects in nationwide ISIS crackdown
headlines.summary: Counterterrorism police captured 110 suspects across Istanbul and other cities in operations targeting Daesh recruitment networks.

perspectives.angle: Turkey Arrests 110 People in Nationwide ISIS Crackdown
perspectives.summary: Turkish counter-terror police detained 110 suspects across the country in simultaneous operations targeting ISIS networks and financing.

headlines.headline: Indonesian rupiah plunges to record low as markets reel
headlines.summary: The Indonesian currency hit an all-time low amid economic pressures, with critics warning of impact on import-dependent citizens.

headlines.headline: Iranian civilians receive assault rifle training amid war fears
headlines.summary: Groups of Iranians are learning to use AK-47 rifles from Revolutionary Guard instructors in central Tehran as domestic preparedness efforts intensify.

headlines.headline: Chinese-trained Russian soldiers return to fight in Ukraine
headlines.summary: European intelligence agencies report that China secretly trained around 200 Russian military personnel late last year, with some now fighting in Ukraine—a significant escalation in Beijing-Moscow military cooperation.

headlines.headline: Russia strikes Ukrainian Danube port as drones target Moscow
headlines.summary: Russia attacked Izmail, Ukraine's largest Danube port, while Ukraine launched drone strikes toward Moscow—escalating cross-border attacks as the war enters a new phase.

perspectives.angle: Ukrainian mid-range drone strikes deal double blow to Russia
perspectives.summary: Ukraine's growing medium-range drone capabilities are burning oil refineries and disrupting Russia's ground offensive—a significant tactical advantage as both sides escalate strikes.

perspectives.angle: Russia strikes Ukrainian Danube port as Kyiv targets Moscow with drones
perspectives.summary: Russian air attacks damaged port infrastructure in Izmail, while Russian authorities downed four Ukrainian drones headed toward Moscow in overnight attacks.

headlines.headline: Russian rouble hits three-year high against yuan before Putin visit
headlines.summary: The Russian rouble strengthened to its highest level against China's yuan since February 2023 ahead of President Putin's visit to Beijing with new business deals on the agenda.

headlines.headline: Taiwan cautiously optimistic on US arms sales
headlines.summary: Taiwan's defence minister expressed cautious optimism about US arms sales, after Trump said he was still considering whether to proceed with new weapons sales to the Chinese-claimed island.

headlines.headline: Ebola deaths in eastern Congo rise to 131 as outbreak spreads
headlines.summary: Twenty-six more suspected Ebola deaths were recorded in 24 hours in eastern Democratic Republic of Congo, bringing the death toll to 131, as the WHO chief expressed deep concern about the outbreak's speed and scale.

perspectives.angle: WHO Warns Ebola Outbreak Spreading Faster Than Expected in DR Congo
perspectives.summary: A WHO official warns the Ebola outbreak in eastern DR Congo may be spreading faster than initially thought, with over 130 suspected deaths and 513 cases reported. The outbreak involves the rare Bundibugyo strain in an area affected by conflict.

perspectives.angle: DR Congo Ebola Death Toll Surges to 131 as WHO Raises Alarm
perspectives.summary: The estimated death toll from the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo has risen sharply to 131, with WHO expressing deep concern over scale and speed.

headlines.headline: Russian missile and drone strikes kill four in Ukraine's northern regions
headlines.summary: Russian ballistic missile and drone strikes killed four people and injured several others in Ukraine's northern Chernihiv and Sumy regions, according to Ukrainian officials, as attacks continued on multiple fronts.

perspectives.angle: Ukrainian Drone Strikes Deal Double Blow to Russian War Effort
perspectives.summary: Medium-range Ukrainian drone attacks on Russian oil refineries have significantly disrupted Russia's war machine, compounding problems from stalled ground offensives and Western sanctions.

perspectives.angle: Ukraine Drone Attack Kills Four in Moscow's Biggest Strike in Over a Year
perspectives.summary: At least four people were killed after Ukraine launched its largest drone attack on Moscow in more than a year, demonstrating Kyiv's ability to strike targets over 300 miles from the border despite dense Russian air defenses.

headlines.headline: Global stocks and bonds steady as oil prices ease on Trump Iran comments
headlines.summary: Global markets stabilized after President Trump paused a planned attack on Iran and said there was a good chance of reaching a nuclear deal, sending oil prices lower and calming investor concerns about escalating Middle East tensions.

perspectives.angle: Oil Prices Fall, Wall Street Hits Record as Iran Reopens Strait of Hormuz
perspectives.summary: Oil prices dropped sharply and US stocks surged to record highs after Iran announced the Strait of Hormuz was open again for commercial tankers. The S&P 500 jumped 1.2% as markets rallied on hopes for de-escalation.

headlines.headline: G7 finance ministers explore responses to Iran war economic fallout
headlines.summary: France's finance minister urged the IMF and World Bank to help countries most vulnerable to Middle East conflict fallout, as G7 finance ministers met in Paris to address rising energy prices and sanctions policy amid the US-Iran standoff.

perspectives.angle: G7 Finance Ministers Meet in Paris Amid Trade Tensions and Iran War Fallout
perspectives.summary: Finance ministers from the Group of Seven nations convene in Paris to address global economic imbalances and coordinate critical raw material supplies. Geopolitical differences over the Iran conflict threaten to test the group's unity as the Strait of Hormuz blockade disrupts energy markets.

headlines.headline: Putin says Russia and China ready to back each other on sovereignty issues
headlines.summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin stated ahead of his visit to Beijing that Russia and China are prepared to support each other on a wide range of issues including national unity and protection of sovereignty, in a video address released before his Tuesday summit with Xi Jinping.

perspectives.angle: Putin Visits China to Reaffirm Russia Ties as Xi Seeks Stability With Both Russia and US
perspectives.summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin meets Xi Jinping in Beijing less than a week after Trump visit. China aims to maintain stable US relations while preserving strong ties with Russia, coinciding with the 25th anniversary of the Sino-Russian Treaty of Friendship.

headlines.headline: Gunman Kills Eight at Egyptian Transport Hub in Southern Province
headlines.summary: A lone gunman killed eight people and wounded five others at a transport hub in Assiut province before being shot dead by security forces.

headlines.headline: Germany Begins Privatization of Bailed-Out Energy Giant Uniper
headlines.summary: The German government announced the sale of its stake in Uniper, the energy company nationalized during the 2022 gas crisis, marking a major step in post-crisis restructuring.

headlines.headline: South Korea and Japan Expand Energy Cooperation Amid Regional Tensions
headlines.summary: South Korea and Japan agreed to expand cooperation on LNG and crude oil supply, including stockpiling and petroleum product swap arrangements, strengthening security ties as both nations face geopolitical challenges in the region.

perspectives.angle: Japan and South Korea deepen energy cooperation amid regional tensions
perspectives.summary: Leaders agreed to strengthen ties on energy security and AI during talks, building on recent diplomatic thaw between the two nations.

headlines.headline: Former Spanish PM Zapatero Investigated for Influence Peddling
headlines.summary: A Spanish court is investigating former Prime Minister José Luis Rodríguez Zapatero for alleged influence peddling and related crimes in connection with an airline bailout during his tenure, adding to political turmoil in Spain.

headlines.headline: Russia Launches Major Nuclear Drills Amid Ukraine War
headlines.summary: Russia began three days of nuclear weapons exercises involving ballistic and cruise missile launches, raising concerns as Belarus simultaneously conducts its own nuclear drills near NATO borders.

perspectives.angle: Russia Launches Nationwide Nuclear Drills as Ukraine Drone War Intensifies
perspectives.summary: Moscow began nationwide nuclear exercises while both Russia and Ukraine escalated cross-border aerial strikes, marking a dangerous escalation in the conflict.

headlines.headline: Iran Proposes Peace Deal With Reparations and US Troop Withdrawal
headlines.summary: Tehran presented a revised peace proposal to the US via Pakistan, demanding reparations for war damage and withdrawal of American forces from the region, as diplomatic efforts continue amid ongoing conflict.

headlines.headline: UK Energy Bills Set to Jump 13% as Iran War Drives Gas Prices
headlines.summary: Britain's household energy price cap is expected to surge 13% this summer, the largest increase since 2023, as the conflict in Iran pushes up wholesale gas and electricity costs.

headlines.headline: Iran Announces New Authority to "Manage" Strait of Hormuz
headlines.summary: Iran formed a new body to oversee the Strait of Hormuz, managing ship transits and collecting passage fees. The move comes as Tehran continues its blockade while facing a US maritime embargo on Iranian ports.

perspectives.angle: Iran Establishes New Body to Manage Strait of Hormuz Amid Stalled Talks
perspectives.summary: Iran formed the Persian Gulf Strait Authority (PGSA) to manage the vital shipping route as peace negotiations with the US remained deadlocked.

headlines.headline: Malaysia PM Anwar Eyes Snap Election Before Fuel Subsidy Crisis Bites
headlines.summary: Malaysian Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim is considering a snap election before fuel subsidy costs—potentially 4 billion ringgit monthly due to the Iran war—force spending cuts that would hurt his chances. His coalition is fraying with partners defecting.

headlines.headline: Belarus Holds Nuclear Weapons Drills with Russia
headlines.summary: Belarus began training exercises involving Russian nuclear weapons, drawing strong condemnation from Ukraine amid escalating regional tensions.

perspectives.angle: Belarus Holds Nuclear Weapons Drills with Russia
perspectives.summary: Belarusian armed forces began training exercises involving Russian nuclear weapons, drawing strong condemnation from Ukraine. The drills come amid heightened tensions over Russian military activities in Belarus.

headlines.headline: US Sanctions Cuban Officials and Intelligence Agency
headlines.summary: The Trump administration imposed sanctions on 11 Cuban officials including the communications minister and military leaders, escalating pressure on the communist government.

headlines.headline: Taiwan Says China Greatest Source of Regional Instability
headlines.summary: Taiwan condemned China as the primary source of regional unease as Beijing sent a carrier task force into the Western Pacific for training exercises.

headlines.headline: WHO Rejects Taiwan Participation After China Opposition
headlines.summary: World Health Organization member states rejected a proposal to invite Taiwan to its annual assembly in Geneva after China said it would block participation.

headlines.headline: US Drops Adani Cases After $10 Billion Investment Promise
headlines.summary: The Trump administration moved to dismiss criminal fraud charges against Indian billionaire Gautam Adani while settling alleged Iran sanctions violations involving one of his companies.

headlines.headline: Japan economy grows faster than expected in Q1 despite Iran war headwinds
headlines.summary: Japan posted stronger-than-expected GDP growth in the first quarter driven by solid exports and consumption, though economists warn the Iran war energy shock will test momentum.

headlines.headline: IMF raises UK growth forecast but warns political turmoil threatens outlook
headlines.summary: The International Monetary Fund upgraded Britain's 2026 growth projection but cautioned that domestic political instability could undermine spending and investment.

headlines.headline: Kremlin dismisses Zelenskiy warning about Russian threat to NATO from Belarus
headlines.summary: Moscow rejected Kyiv's assertion that Russia was considering an attack on NATO countries from Belarusian territory, calling the claim unworthy of response.

headlines.headline: Mexico Demands Israel Respect Rights of Gaza Flotilla Nationals
headlines.summary: Mexico called on Israeli authorities to respect the human rights of two Mexican nationals detained after the Israeli Navy intercepted a Gaza-bound aid flotilla. A South Korean activist was also detained.

perspectives.angle: Israel Intercepts Gaza Flotilla Carrying Irish President's Sister
perspectives.summary: Israeli forces intercepted a Gaza-bound aid flotilla carrying the sister of Irish President Catherine Connolly and other activists. Ten nations condemned the interception, with Ireland and Mexico demanding their citizens be treated fairly.

perspectives.angle: Israeli Navy Intercepts Gaza-Bound Flotilla in International Waters
perspectives.summary: Israeli naval forces boarded multiple civilian vessels from the Global Sumud Flotilla carrying humanitarian aid activists, detaining participants and diverting ships to Ashdod port in enforcement of the Gaza maritime blockade.

headlines.headline: Taiwan President Lai Survives First Impeachment Vote
headlines.summary: An impeachment motion against Taiwanese President William Lai failed on Tuesday, marking the first presidential impeachment vote in Taiwans history. The motion was brought by opposition lawmakers.

headlines.headline: Cuba Warns of Bloodbath After US Drone Sanctions
headlines.summary: Cuban President Diaz-Canel warned of a bloodbath if the US attacks, after Washington imposed sanctions following reports Havana obtained over 300 military drones. Cuba accused the US of fabricating drone threat claims.

headlines.headline: Philippines Would Be Involved in Taiwan Conflict, Marcos Says
headlines.summary: Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. acknowledged that due to geographic proximity, the Philippines would likely be involved in any Taiwan conflict. He also said he would discuss Japans defense posture at an upcoming summit amid Taiwan concerns.

perspectives.angle: Marcos warns Philippines would be drawn into any Taiwan conflict
perspectives.summary: Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. stated that the Philippines would inevitably be involved in any conflict over Taiwan due to geographic proximity, ahead of security talks with Japanese PM Sanae Takaichi in Tokyo.

headlines.headline: IDF Intercepts Hezbollah Drone Over South Lebanon
headlines.summary: Israeli forces fired an interceptor missile at an apparent Hezbollah drone over southern Lebanon where troops were operating. Israel continues airstrikes in southern Lebanon despite a truce extension.

headlines.headline: Ukrainian Positions in Pokrovsk Nearly Cut Off
headlines.summary: Ukraines 7th Rapid Response Corps says Russian forces control high ground and skies over northern Pokrovsk, making troop rotations and resupply nearly impossible. Analysts say those positions should have been withdrawn months ago.

perspectives.angle: Ukrainian troops nearly encircled in Pokrovsk as Russian pressure mounts
perspectives.summary: Ukrainian forces holding positions in northern Pokrovsk are "almost cut off" as Russian forces dominate skies, logistics routes, and high ground around the embattled Donetsk city, according to Ukraine's 7th Rapid Response Corps.

headlines.headline: Xi to Host Putin in Beijing Days After Trump Visit
headlines.summary: Chinese leader Xi Jinping is set to host Vladimir Putin less than a week after Donald Trump visited Beijing, as China seeks to project itself as a stable global power amid war and energy crisis. The visit underscores Beijing balancing act between Russia and US.

perspectives.angle: Putin arrives in Beijing to reinforce China ties after Trump summit
perspectives.summary: The Russian president meets Xi Jinping just days after Trump's high-profile Beijing visit, as China seeks to project itself as a stable global power amid multiple crises.

headlines.headline: Pakistan Deploys 8,000 Troops and Fighter Jets to Saudi Arabia
headlines.summary: Pakistan has deployed around 8,000 troops, a squadron of fighter jets and an air defense system to Saudi Arabia under a mutual defense pact during the Iran conflict. The deployment complicates Pakistans role as a neutral mediator in the Iran-US tensions.

headlines.headline: US SEC Prepares Framework for Trading Tokenized Stocks
headlines.summary: The Trump administration plans to unveil a framework for trading tokenized or digital versions of securities, Bloomberg News reported. The move would create new regulatory structure for crypto-based stock trading.

headlines.headline: Pentagon Watchdog to Investigate US Boat Strikes in Caribbean
headlines.summary: The Pentagons internal watchdog will investigate whether US attacks on boats in the Caribbean followed targeting guidelines, amid claims from outside groups that the strikes were illegal.

headlines.headline: Analog Devices in Talks to Buy Empower Semiconductor for $1.5 Billion
headlines.summary: US chipmaker Analog Devices is in advanced talks to acquire AI chip company Empower Semiconductor for about $1.5 billion in cash. The deal would expand Analog Devices presence in the AI semiconductor market.

headlines.headline: Trump Pauses Iran Strike, Says Nuclear Deal Possible
headlines.summary: President Trump announced he has put an attack on Iran on hold at the request of Gulf allies and believes there is a "very good chance" of reaching a nuclear deal with Tehran.

perspectives.angle: Trump Calls Off Iran Strike After Gulf Allies Request Delay
perspectives.summary: US President Donald Trump announced he postponed a planned military strike on Iran scheduled for Tuesday, stating that Gulf allies requested the delay as serious negotiations are taking place. The decision comes amid heightened tensions in the Middle East.

headlines.headline: US Extends Sanctions Waiver on Russian Oil for Vulnerable Countries
headlines.summary: The US Treasury has extended a sanctions waiver allowing Russian seaborne oil to reach vulnerable countries, as the Iran conflict squeezes global crude supplies.

perspectives.angle: Dollar retreats as oil falls on report of US-Iran sanctions waiver
perspectives.summary: The dollar slipped against major currencies as oil prices fell after an Iranian news report indicated the US may waive sanctions on Iranian crude. 10-year Treasury yields withdrew from 15-month highs.

headlines.headline: Russia Unleashes Aerial Barrage on Ukraine as Long-Range Strikes Escalate
headlines.summary: Russia launched a major aerial attack on Ukraine with drones and missiles, as both sides escalate long-range strikes in the ongoing war.

headlines.headline: US Pressure Campaign Not Moving Iran, Diplomatic Path Uncertain
headlines.summary: Analysis shows the Trump administration pressure strategy on Iran is not achieving its goals as Tehran resists demands while the conflict risks escalating further.

headlines.headline: Japan stands ready to intervene on yen volatility amid global bond selloff
headlines.summary: Japanese officials at G7 talks in Paris said Tokyo is prepared to act against excessive forex volatility at any time, while being mindful of the impact on US Treasury yields from any yen-buying intervention.

headlines.headline: Middle East war crushes tourism as Europe becomes alternative destination
headlines.summary: The Iran conflict has led to sharp drops in tourist visits to Middle East destinations including Jordan's Petra and Tunisia's Djerba island, with European destinations benefiting as travelers stay closer to home.

headlines.headline: Erdogan warns Iran conflict devastating global economy like a tsunami
headlines.summary: Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan said the Iran conflict is crushing the global economy with shockwaves spreading worldwide, creating speculative volatility that makes accurate damage assessment impossible.

headlines.headline: Russia hammers Kyiv for third straight day, flattening apartment block
headlines.summary: Russian forces launched mass drone and missile attacks on Kyiv for the third consecutive day, damaging six districts and injuring civilians. The escalation follows Ukraine's largest drone attack on Moscow this year.

headlines.headline: US pauses WWII-era joint defense board with Canada over commitments dispute
headlines.summary: The Pentagon announced it is pausing participation in the Permanent Joint Board on Defense with Canada, accusing Ottawa of failing to meet defense commitments. The board has coordinated continental defense since 1940.

headlines.headline: Poland seeks urgent talks with US after troop deployment halt
headlines.summary: Poland's defense minister called for honest discussions with Washington after being blindsided by the US decision to halt troop deployments. The move raises concerns about NATO eastern flank security.

headlines.headline: Xi told Trump that Putin might regret Ukraine invasion
headlines.summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping told Trump during their Beijing summit that Putin may come to regret the invasion of Ukraine, according to US assessments. The disclosure suggests growing Chinese unease over Russia's war.

headlines.headline: Trump Threatens Iran as Middle East Tensions Surge
headlines.summary: President Trump has sought to force Iran to accept his terms on its nuclear program or face renewed war, as an emboldened Israel weighs its next moves. The administration is pushing for a deal while military options remain on the table.

headlines.headline: Oil Prices Drop Over 2% After Trump Delays Iran Attack
headlines.summary: Global oil prices fell more than 2% in early Asian trade after President Trump announced he had paused a planned military strike on Iran. The delay signals potential de-escalation and revived hopes for diplomatic negotiations.

perspectives.angle: Oil prices climb after drone attack on UAE nuclear plant
perspectives.summary: Oil prices extended gains as efforts to end the US-Iran conflict appeared stalled following a drone attack on a UAE nuclear power plant and President Trump prepared to discuss military options on Iran.

headlines.headline: Putin Visits China to Reaffirm Russia Ties as Xi Seeks Stable US Relations
headlines.summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin is traveling to China to meet with Chinese leader Xi Jinping. The visit comes less than a week after the Trump-Xi summit, as Beijing balances its partnership with Moscow against improving ties with Washington.

perspectives.angle: Putin-Xi summit to advance Russia-China ties amid Iran war
perspectives.summary: Beijing becomes a global focal point as Russia pins hopes on the upcoming Putin-Xi summit, with the Power of Siberia 2 gas pipeline expected to be discussed in detail.

headlines.headline: Trump Moves to Admit 10,000 More White South Africans as Refugees
headlines.summary: The Trump administration argues that an "emergency refugee situation" in South Africa merits bringing more Afrikaners to America, at an estimated cost of some $100 million. Critics call the move politically motivated.

headlines.headline: Japan considers unmanned early-warning aircraft for Pacific defense
headlines.summary: Japan is considering deploying unmanned aircraft equipped with airborne early-warning radar systems as part of efforts to strengthen Pacific defense capabilities amid regional tensions.

headlines.headline: Hegseth campaigns for congressional candidate, breaks Pentagon neutrality
headlines.summary: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth campaigned for a congressional challenger endorsed by President Trump, breaking with the traditional Pentagon stance of political neutrality.

headlines.headline: Indonesia receives new armaments to defend sovereignty
headlines.summary: President Prabowo Subianto handed over replica keys to new military armaments, emphasizing the equipment aims to defend Indonesian sovereignty and strengthen national defense.

headlines.headline: US pauses WWII-era joint defense board with Canada amid tensions
headlines.summary: The United States suspended participation in a long-standing joint defense forum with Canada that dates to World War II, citing concerns amid growing bilateral tensions.

headlines.headline: Five Dead in San Diego Mosque Shooting, Suspects Found Dead
headlines.summary: Two teenage gunmen opened fire at the Islamic Center of San Diego, killing three men including a security guard before being found dead from self-inflicted gunshot wounds. Police are investigating the attack as a potential hate crime.

perspectives.angle: Teenage Gunmen Kill Three at San Diego Mosque in Suspected Hate Crime
perspectives.summary: Two teenage attackers fatally shot three men at the Islamic Center of San Diego before taking their own lives, in what authorities are investigating as a possible hate crime. A security guard is being hailed as a hero for saving lives during the attack.

headlines.headline: Earthquake in Southwest China Kills Two, Evacuates 7,000
headlines.summary: A magnitude 5.2 earthquake struck China's Guangxi region, killing at least two people and forcing over 7,000 evacuations in Liuzhou city. Search and rescue operations continue amid warnings of transport disruptions.

perspectives.angle: China Earthquake Kills Two, Forces Thousands to Evacuate
perspectives.summary: A magnitude 5.2 earthquake in China's southwest Guangxi region killed two people and forced more than 7,000 in the city of Liuzhou to evacuate. Authorities warn of transport disruptions as search and rescue operations continue.

headlines.headline: OpenAI Defeats Elon Musk Lawsuit, Clearing Path to IPO
headlines.summary: A U.S. jury ruled against Elon Musk in his lawsuit against OpenAI, finding the AI company not liable for allegedly straying from its original mission. Musk's lawyers said they plan to appeal the verdict.

headlines.headline: Trump Drops IRS Lawsuit in Exchange for $1.8B 'Weaponization' Fund
headlines.summary: President Trump dismissed his $10 billion lawsuit against the IRS after the Justice Department agreed to create a fund controlled by his allies to compensate those who claim to have suffered "weaponization or lawfare" by the government.

headlines.headline: TrumpRx Site to List 600 Generic Drugs as Trump Targets Lower Prices
headlines.summary: The Trump administration will announce a dramatic expansion of TrumpRx.gov, a government-backed website offering discounted prescription drugs, as part of efforts to lower healthcare costs for Americans.

headlines.headline: Defense Secretary Hegseth Campaigns Against Trump GOP Foe in Kentucky
headlines.summary: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth made a rare wartime visit to the campaign trail, urging Kentucky Republicans to defeat Rep. Thomas Massie and send Trump "reinforcements" who pledge to support his agenda.

headlines.headline: CDC: One American Tested Positive for Ebola in Congo Outbreak
headlines.summary: U.S. health officials announced steps to reduce Ebola risk amid concerns over a new outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo, where a rare strain with no approved therapeutics has killed nearly 120 people.

perspectives.angle: WHO Declares Ebola Global Health Emergency in Congo-Uganda Outbreak
perspectives.summary: The World Health Organization declared the Ebola outbreak in Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda a public health emergency of international concern after more than 300 suspected cases and 88 deaths. The CDC confirmed Americans are among those affected.

headlines.headline: UAE Nuclear Plant Struck by Drone in Attack Sparking Regional Fears
headlines.summary: A drone strike sparked a fire at the edge of the United Arab Emirates' sole nuclear power plant at Barakah, raising fears of a dangerous new phase in the Gulf conflict. No one claimed responsibility, and UAE authorities called it an "unprovoked terrorist attack."

perspectives.angle: Drone strike targets UAE nuclear power plant amid Iran conflict tensions
perspectives.summary: A drone strike hit the Barakah nuclear power plant in the UAE, setting an electrical generator on fire. No one claimed responsibility, but suspicion fell on Iran as regional tensions remain high. Nuclear safety experts warn that nuclear facilities must not be targets in conflicts.

headlines.headline: US Pauses WWII-Era Defense Board with Canada Over Spending Dispute
headlines.summary: The Pentagon announced it is pausing participation in the Permanent Joint Board on Defense with Canada, a bilateral forum dating back to World War II, accusing Ottawa of failing to make credible progress on defense commitments amid rising tensions over military spending.

headlines.headline: Closed-Door US-Greenland Talks Reveal Trump's Arctic Ambitions
headlines.summary: Greenlandic officials expressed concern about the direction of negotiations with Washington as the US pushes for a major role in the Arctic territory. The island's government says progress has been made but insists Greenland will never be for sale.

perspectives.angle: US demands major role in Greenland through closed-door negotiations
perspectives.summary: Confidential talks between the US, Greenland, and Denmark reveal American demands for indefinite military presence and veto power over major investment deals in Greenland, aimed at countering Russian and Chinese influence in the Arctic. Greenlandic officials fear the demands would compromise their sovereignty for generations.

headlines.headline: Syria Joins G7 Finance Sessions in Paris Sign of Growing Status
headlines.summary: Syria will participate in a closed-door session with G7 finance ministers and central bank governors in Paris, marking a significant step in its international rehabilitation less than two years after the ousting of Bashar al-Assad.

perspectives.angle: Syria Joins G7 Finance Sessions in Paris in Sign of Legitimization
perspectives.summary: Syria will participate in closed-door G7 finance minister meetings in Paris, a sign of its growing international status less than two years after the fall of Assad.

headlines.headline: Ukraine develops first homegrown guided aerial bomb
headlines.summary: Ukraine has declared its domestically-produced 250kg guided aerial bomb combat-ready, reducing dependence on Western precision strike capabilities and providing an answer to Russia's daily glide-bomb attacks.

headlines.headline: US sanctions senior Cuban officials as tensions escalate
headlines.summary: The US sanctioned 11 people including top Cuban officials, generals and intelligence officers, targeting the island's justice ministry and other key figures amid rising tensions.

headlines.headline: Iran War Tests Dollar-Dominated Oil Trading System
headlines.summary: The global oil trading system centered on the U.S. dollar is being tested by the Iran war and Strait of Hormuz closure, as major consuming nations turn to increasingly opaque deals with Tehran and Gulf producers to secure supplies.

perspectives.angle: US extends Russian oil sanctions waiver to aid nations hit by Iran war
perspectives.summary: The US Treasury announced another 30-day extension of sanctions waiver allowing purchases of Russian oil, helping countries most affected by disruptions from the Iran conflict.

perspectives.angle: US Extends Russian Oil Sanctions Waiver Amid Iran War Disruption
perspectives.summary: The US Treasury announced a 30-day extension of sanctions waivers allowing purchases of Russian seaborne oil to help energy-vulnerable countries cope with supply disruptions caused by the Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: Israel-Hezbollah Fighting Continues Despite Extended Truce
headlines.summary: Israel conducted airstrikes in southern Lebanon while Hezbollah announced new attacks on Israeli forces, with the death toll in Lebanon surpassing 3,000. The violence persists despite a U.S.-backed truce extension.

perspectives.angle: Israel-Hezbollah fighting continues despite truce extension
perspectives.summary: Israel carried out airstrikes in southern Lebanon while Hezbollah announced new attacks on Israeli forces, continuing the conflict despite a U.S.-backed truce extension. Lebanon death toll nears 3,000.

headlines.headline: Iran War Costs Global Companies Over $25 Billion and Counting
headlines.summary: Businesses worldwide are grappling with soaring energy prices, fractured supply chains, and severed trade routes as Iran maintains its chokehold on the Strait of Hormuz, with the economic toll mounting daily.

perspectives.angle: Global Bond Rout Deepens as Iran War Stokes Worldwide Inflation Fears
perspectives.summary: Government bonds from Tokyo to New York extended losses as rising energy prices from the Iran conflict fanned inflation fears and stoked investor wagers on rate hikes from central banks around the world.

perspectives.angle: Rising Diesel Costs from Iran War Strain US School Budgets
perspectives.summary: Soaring diesel prices since the onset of the Iran war are draining already tight U.S. school district budgets, forcing some to draw from emergency financial reserves to cover transportation costs.

headlines.headline: Global Bond Rout Deepens as Inflation Fears Mount
headlines.summary: Sovereign bond yields are racing to decade highs as investors wake up to the risk that the Iran war may deliver a lasting inflationary shock, threatening government, business, and household spending power worldwide.

headlines.headline: Iran Creates New Body to Manage Strait of Hormuz as Talks Stall
headlines.summary: Iran announced a new oversight body for the strategic Strait of Hormuz waterway, signaling tighter control as peace talks with the US remain deadlocked and the risk of renewed conflict rises.

headlines.headline: Cuba warns of bloodbath if US attacks as Washington imposes sanctions
headlines.summary: President Diaz-Canel warned of a bloodbath if the US attacks Cuba, while the US Treasury sanctioned Cuba's main intelligence agency and top leaders amid reports Havana obtained military drones from Russia and Iran.

headlines.headline: US asks Israel to keep refueling aircraft at Ben Gurion through 2026
headlines.summary: The US has requested Israel to keep its refueling aircraft at Ben Gurion Airport until the end of 2026, with reports indicating the presence of US aircraft is causing operational difficulties at the airport.

headlines.headline: Fifty countries back Taiwan's WHA participation bid despite Chinese opposition
headlines.summary: Over 50 countries have expressed support for Taiwan's participation in the World Health Assembly and other international organizations, while the WHO rejected a proposal to invite Taiwan after China vowed to block participation.

headlines.headline: Philippine Senate presidency fight could shape Sara Duterte impeachment trial
headlines.summary: The Philippine Senate convened as an impeachment court for Vice-President Sara Duterte amid a leadership battle that could influence her trial and the fallout from her father's ICC case.

perspectives.angle: Philippine VP Sara Duterte Faces Senate Impeachment Trial
perspectives.summary: Philippine Vice President Sara Duterte faces a senate impeachment trial on charges including bribery, corruption, and threatening the president's life. The outcome could reshape the 2028 presidential race and determine the Duterte clan's future in national politics.

headlines.headline: Pakistan Deploys Fighter Jets and 8,000 Troops to Saudi Arabia Amid Iran War
headlines.summary: Pakistan has stationed a fighter squadron and thousands of soldiers in Saudi Arabia during the Iran conflict, deepening military cooperation with Riyadh while simultaneously mediating between Tehran and Washington.

perspectives.angle: Pakistan Deploys 8,000 Troops and Fighter Jets to Saudi Arabia Amid Iran War
perspectives.summary: While mediating between Iran and the US, Pakistan has secretly deployed 8,000 troops, a JF-17 fighter squadron, drones, and Chinese HQ-9 air defense systems to Saudi Arabia after the kingdom faced drone attacks from Iraqi airspace.

headlines.headline: Greenland Says US Talks Make Progress But Island Is Not For Sale
headlines.summary: Greenlands government reports progress in high-stakes talks with the United States over the territorys future, while firmly rejecting any possibility of sale despite President Trumps persistent interest in acquiring the strategic Arctic territory.

headlines.headline: Russian Forces Strike Odesa and Dnipro With Missiles and Drones
headlines.summary: Russia attacked the Ukrainian cities of Odesa and Dnipro with missiles and drones, damaging residential buildings and injuring dozens, while Ukrainian forces struck the Belgorod region, killing two people according to Russian officials.

headlines.headline: France's Macron Kicks Off Kenya Visit for Africa Forward Summit
headlines.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron began a visit to Kenya ahead of the Africa Forward Summit, showcasing France's new policy for the continent - a shift from colonial power to partnership of equals amid changing global geopolitics.

perspectives.angle: France Pitches New Africa Partnership Model at Kenya Summit
perspectives.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron opened the Africa Forward Summit in Nairobi, France's first major summit in an English-speaking African country, promoting a "partnership of equals" as Paris pivots away from its troubled relationship with former colonies.

headlines.headline: Hamas Military Wing Leader Killed in Israeli Airstrike
headlines.summary: Israel confirmed killing Izz al-Din al-Haddad, one of the last surviving architects of the October 7 attacks, in a Gaza airstrike. Hamas confirmed the death amid an ongoing fragile ceasefire.

headlines.headline: First Russian Student Recruited From University Dies in Ukraine
headlines.summary: Valery Averin, 23, became the first known Russian recruited directly from a university to die in Ukraine, exposing Moscow's campaign to recruit students into military service through financial incentives.

headlines.headline: Australia Orders Chinese Investors to Divest From Rare Earths Company
headlines.summary: The Australian government ordered Chinese investors to divest from Northern Minerals, a rare earths company. Analysts say the move reflects efforts to diversify supply chains away from China but risks Beijing retaliation.

headlines.headline: Bolivia faces largest protests in decades as Evo Morales supporters join movement
headlines.summary: Followers of former President Evo Morales have joined massive protests against President Rodrigo Paz, marking the biggest challenge to Bolivia's first conservative leader after nearly two decades of socialist rule. The protests are fueled by the nation's worst economic crisis in a generation.

headlines.headline: US-Iran Standoff Deepens as Neither Side Bends
headlines.summary: Three months after the US-Israel attack on Iran, a US blockade and Tehran's grip on the Strait of Hormuz have created a deadlock with neither side willing to compromise, raising the risk of renewed war.

headlines.headline: Opaque Oil Deals Around Hormuz Test the Petrodollar
headlines.summary: The US dollar-dominated global oil trading system is being tested by the Iran war and Strait of Hormuz closure, as governments turn to increasingly opaque deals with Tehran and Gulf producers to secure supplies.

headlines.headline: India's Cooking Fuel Shortage Driving Up California Gas Prices
headlines.summary: A cooking gas shortage in India and $6 per gallon gasoline in California are both symptoms of the worst-ever energy supply disruption caused by the Iran war, demonstrating the global interconnectedness of fuel markets.

headlines.headline: Indonesian Rupiah Hits Record Low Despite Central Bank Intervention
headlines.summary: The Indonesian rupiah slid to another record low despite central bank interventions, as global oil prices surged to two-week highs, though President Prabowo downplayed the impact on citizens.

headlines.headline: China Rejects Taiwan Sovereignty Claims After Leader Pledges Never to Be Sacrificed
headlines.summary: Beijing stated Taiwan was "never a country" after Taiwanese leader William Lai Ching-te said the island would never be traded away, in the latest exchange over cross-strait sovereignty.

headlines.headline: Germany Calls for Stronger EU-Turkey Ties, Says Ankara Key to NATO Defense
headlines.summary: German Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul said Turkey should be included as the EU develops its defense policy, citing shared security interests and Turkiye role as a critical NATO ally.

headlines.headline: Israel Opposition Demands Knesset Dissolution, Early Elections Amid Coalition Strains
headlines.summary: Israeli opposition leaders urged lawmakers to support a bill for preliminary vote next week to dissolve parliament, as the governing coalition faces mounting internal political pressure.

headlines.headline: SCMP Covers Belarus Nuclear Exercises Near NATO Borders
headlines.summary: Belarus began training exercises involving Russian tactical nuclear weapons, testing military readiness to deploy nuclear arms across different regions of the country bordering three NATO states.

headlines.headline: Pakistan Conveys New Iranian Peace Proposal to United States
headlines.summary: Iran sent a revised peace proposal to the US via Pakistani mediators with terms similar to previously rejected offers, though a senior Iranian official claims Washington has softened on some issues.

headlines.headline: China Commits to $17 Billion in Annual US Agricultural Purchases
headlines.summary: Following the Trump-Xi summit in Beijing, China agreed to buy at least $17 billion annually in US farm products for three years, marking a significant trade commitment amid broader diplomatic engagement.

perspectives.angle: China agrees to buy at least $17 billion in US agricultural products annually
perspectives.summary: The White House announced that China has committed to purchasing at least $17 billion in US agricultural products annually following the Beijing summit between Trump and Xi Jinping. The agreement represents a significant trade concession from Beijing amid ongoing tariff tensions.

perspectives.angle: China Commits to B Annual US Agricultural Purchases Through 2028
perspectives.summary: China has agreed to purchase at least  billion of US agricultural products annually through 2028, the White House announced, offering stability to American farmers after Phase One trade deal.

headlines.headline: Russia Strikes Odesa and Dnipro in Overnight Attacks
headlines.summary: Russian forces targeted Ukrainian cities of Odesa and Dnipro with missiles and drones, damaging residential buildings and injuring dozens, while Ukrainian drone strikes killed two in Russia.

headlines.headline: Kremlin Has High Expectations for Putin's China Visit
headlines.summary: Russia expressed serious expectations for President Putin's upcoming trip to China this week, where he will meet Xi Jinping to discuss developing their "privileged partnership" amid the Iran war and Ukraine conflict.

headlines.headline: Ukraine and Hungary to Hold Minority Rights Consultations
headlines.summary: Kyiv and Budapest agreed to talks about Ukraine's ethnic Hungarian minority rights this week, seeking to rebuild ties following Hungary's government change.

headlines.headline: Putin Faces Difficult Choices as Sanctions Bite, Estonia Spy Chief Says
headlines.summary: Estonia's foreign intelligence chief said Russia has few good options in Ukraine as Western sanctions chip away at its resources while its forces cannot advance significantly on the battlefield.

headlines.headline: Croatia Rejects Proposed Israeli Ambassador Over Government Policies
headlines.summary: Croatian President Milanović says the nominee will not receive approval, citing actions of the current Israeli government and a diplomatic protocol dispute.

headlines.headline: Merkel Criticizes EU for Not Talking to Russia on Ukraine
headlines.summary: Former German Chancellor Angela Merkel said the EU should maintain dialogue with Russia, though she declined to lead such talks herself, in a rare political intervention.

headlines.headline: Greenland PM Tells US Envoy: Self-Determination Cannot Be Negotiated
headlines.summary: Greenland Prime Minister Jens-Frederik Nielsen met with US special envoy Jeff Landry and reaffirmed that Greenland self-determination is not for negotiation.

headlines.headline: Senior Islamic State Leader Killed in US-Nigeria Joint Operation
headlines.summary: Abu-Bilal al-Minuki, a senior Islamic State leader and head of the General Directorate of States, was killed along with several lieutenants in a strike on his compound in Lake Chad Basin. He was linked to the 2018 Dapchi schoolgirls kidnapping.

perspectives.angle: Senior Islamic State leader killed in US-Nigeria joint operation
perspectives.summary: US and Nigerian forces killed Abu-Bilal al-Minuki, described by President Trump as ISIS's second-in-command globally, in a precision strike on his compound in the Lake Chad Basin. Nigerian President Tinubu called it the most consequential counterterrorism outcome since regional operations began in 2015.

headlines.headline: USAID Cuts Linked to Surge in Violence Across Africa, Study Finds
headlines.summary: A study published in Science journal found that the abrupt dissolution of USAID last year was followed by a significant increase in violence in African countries that had historically received the most support from the agency.

headlines.headline: Zelensky's Former Chief of Staff Released on $3 Million Bail
headlines.summary: Andriy Yermak, former head of Ukraine's presidential office, was released on $3.1 million bail amid a major corruption probe involving money laundering tied to the construction of a luxury cottage complex near Kyiv.

headlines.headline: Macron in Kenya for Africa Summit as France Seeks New Continental Partnership
headlines.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron kicked off a visit to Kenya for the Africa Forward Summit, showcasing France shift from colonial-era dominance toward partnership with African nations. The summit comes amid Frances withdrawal of troops from West Africa.

headlines.headline: Pope Leo to Release First Encyclical on Artificial Intelligence May 25
headlines.summary: Pope Leo will release his first in-depth document on May 25, expected to address the rise of artificial intelligence and challenges to workers rights. The encyclical Magnifica Humanitas will be launched with Anthropic co-founder Christopher Olah.

perspectives.angle: Pope Leo XIV to release first encyclical on AI with Anthropic co-founder
perspectives.summary: Pope Leo XIV will launch his first encyclical on May 25 addressing human dignity in the AI era, joined by Anthropic co-founder. The document will tackle ethical challenges posed by artificial intelligence.

headlines.headline: China Economy Slows Sharply as Industrial Output and Retail Sales Disappoint
headlines.summary: China growth lost momentum in April, with industrial output cooling and retail sales sinking to over three-year lows as the second-largest economy grapples with higher energy costs from the Iran war and persistently weak domestic demand.

perspectives.angle: China economic growth stalls as output and retail sales miss forecasts
perspectives.summary: Industrial output and retail sales sharply undershot expectations in April as the world second-biggest economy wrestled with higher energy costs from the Iran war and persistently weak domestic demand.

headlines.headline: Kenya Paralyzed by Nationwide Fuel Protests as Iran War Drives Price Surge
headlines.summary: Protests over fuel price hikes erupted in Kenyan towns, with a nationwide public transport strike stranding commuters and forcing many to walk to work. Demonstrators lit bonfires and barricaded roads amid anger over record fuel costs.

perspectives.angle: Comoros suspends fuel price hikes after deadly Iran-war protests
perspectives.summary: The East African island nation backed down on fuel price increases triggered by the Iran conflict after protests turned deadly across the archipelago.

headlines.headline: Hantavirus-Hit Cruise Ship Docks in Netherlands for Disinfection After Deadly Outbreak
headlines.summary: A luxury polar explorer ship arrived at Rotterdam port where authorities quarantined 27 remaining crew and planned to cremate a German passenger who died. The MV Hondius had been at the center of a deadly hantavirus outbreak during its seven-week voyage.

headlines.headline: IEA Warns Commercial Oil Inventories Depleting Rapidly
headlines.summary: IEA chief Fatih Birol said commercial oil inventories are depleting rapidly with only weeks worth left due to the Iran war and Strait of Hormuz closure to shipping.

headlines.headline: Cuba Denies Seeking Russian Drones, Accuses US of Building Case for Intervention
headlines.summary: Havana's foreign minister rejected a report that Cuba acquired attack drones from Russia, calling it a "fraudulent case" to justify US aggression.

headlines.headline: Bulgaria Wins Eurovision 2026, Israel Takes Second Place
headlines.summary: Bulgaria's Dara won Eurovision 2026 with her song "Bangaranga," while Israel finished second with 343 points in a competition held under the shadow of regional conflict.

headlines.headline: Israel Built Secret Military Bases in Iraq Before Iran War
headlines.summary: Israel spent over a year preparing covert military sites in western Iraq before launching operations against Iran, according to New York Times reporting. Iraqi officials later confirmed the existence of a second base.

perspectives.angle: US and Israel Prepare for Possible Renewal of War with Iran
perspectives.summary: US military cargo planes and aerial refueling jets have been visible over Tel Aviv daily since the ceasefire, as both nations prepare for potential resumption of full-scale conflict with Iran.

perspectives.angle: US-Israel Preparing for Possible Renewal of Strikes on Iran, Report Says
perspectives.summary: Haaretz reports preparations underway for potential new strikes against Iranian targets as diplomatic negotiations remain stalled.

headlines.headline: Belarus Begins Nuclear Weapons Training Exercise With Russia
headlines.summary: Under the leadership of the Belarusian Chief of General Staff, military units began training for combat use of nuclear weapons and nuclear support, working with Russian forces on delivery and preparation of nuclear munitions.

headlines.headline: Putin Agrees to NATO-Style Security Guarantees for Ukraine
headlines.summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin agreed at his summit with Trump that the US and European allies could offer Ukraine Article 5-like security protections as part of a potential peace deal. Trump envoy Steve Witkoff called it game-changing and a major shift in Russia's position.

headlines.headline: Major Media Outlets Demand Israel Allow Independent Journalists Into Gaza
headlines.summary: Leaders of major media organizations are urging Israel to lift a ban preventing foreign journalists from independently entering Gaza, citing press freedom and public's right to know.

headlines.headline: Australian Workers to Strike at Inpex LNG Facility Amid Global Energy Crisis
headlines.summary: A union in Australia announced workers will strike at Inpex's Ichthys LNG facility from May 27, a move that could worsen already tight global energy supplies as Middle East tensions continue.

headlines.headline: Chinese scientists land on disputed Sandy Cay reef as Philippines tensions escalate
headlines.summary: Beijing researchers landed on the uninhabited South China Sea sandbank over the weekend in the latest assertion of sovereignty claims, amid ongoing territorial disputes with Manila.

headlines.headline: Austrian jets intercept US military aircraft two days in a row amid airspace tensions
headlines.summary: Austria scrambled fighter jets to intercept US military planes for the second consecutive day, highlighting growing friction over military flight operations in European airspace.

headlines.headline: Israels F-35s to get external fuel tanks after extensive Iran combat operations
headlines.summary: Fresh off combat operations against Iran, Israel is boosting the F-35I Adirs endurance with external fuel tanks to reduce dependence on tanker aircraft for long-range missions.

headlines.headline: Middle East oil crisis has already cost global businesses 25 billion dollars
headlines.summary: The economic impact of disrupted Hormuz shipments is mounting rapidly, with Brent crude above 111 and companies worldwide scrambling to secure alternative supply chains.

perspectives.angle: $200 Oil No Longer Unthinkable as Middle East Supply Collapses
perspectives.summary: Middle East oil exports have fallen by 7-10 million bpd, creating severe physical shortages. Analysts warn prices could surge to $150-$200+ per barrel if disruptions persist, with recovery expected to be slow even if conflict ends.

headlines.headline: Cuba Seeks Russian Drones, Eyes Guantanamo Attack
headlines.summary: US intelligence reports Cuba has purchased 300+ Russian and Iranian drones and is discussing attacks on Guantanamo Bay and Key West, with Iranian military advisors now in Havana.

headlines.headline: Turkish Arms Subsidiary Rebrands After Israel Sales Backlash
headlines.summary: Repkon USA, a Turkish-owned defense contractor, rebranded to Paligen Technologies after revelations it would supply bomb bodies for Israel, sparking protests in Istanbul and parliamentary inquiries in Turkey.

headlines.headline: Ethiopia-Eritrea War Fears as Tigray Tensions Escalate
headlines.summary: TPLF leader Debretsion Gebremichael reclaimed presidency of Tigray region, challenging the interim administration. Ethiopian fighter jets seen over Mekelle as fears grow of renewed conflict with Eritrea.

headlines.headline: Zelensky Touts Shift in Battlefield Balance After Moscow Strike
headlines.summary: Following Ukraines largest drone attack on Moscow in over a year, Zelensky announced a shift in momentum and promised more deep strikes as Russia downed over 3,000 drones in the past week.

headlines.headline: Hungary Opposition Leads Orbán for First Time in 15 Years
headlines.summary: Péter Magyars Tisza party leads Viktor Orbáns Fidesz by 12 points ahead of April elections in what Politico calls the EUs most consequential election of 2026.

headlines.headline: Climate Change Reducing Oxygen Levels in Rivers Worldwide, Scientists Find
headlines.summary: A new study reveals that global warming is systematically reducing dissolved oxygen in rivers across the globe, creating expanding "dead zones" that threaten aquatic ecosystems and drinking water supplies.

headlines.headline: German prosecutors expose Russian sanctions-busting network supplying military tech
headlines.summary: A four-year investigation uncovered a Lübeck-based trading firm that routed dual-use components through Turkey into Russia, violating Western sanctions to supply the Kremlin war machine.

headlines.headline: Brent crude breaks $111 as Gulf drone attacks and Hormuz fears spark shortage concerns
headlines.summary: Oil prices surged in early Asian trading after drone strikes on UAE and Saudi Arabia, combined with failed Iran diplomacy, raised fears of global supply disruptions. WTI hit $108.20 while Brent climbed to $111.50.

headlines.headline: Xi and Trump agree Iran must not obtain nuclear weapons in strategic shift on Gulf security
headlines.summary: China and the US reached a rare point of agreement on Iran policy during Trumps China visit, with both leaders signaling that Tehrans nuclear ambitions must be contained, reshaping Middle East power dynamics.

headlines.headline: NATO Defense Ministers Meet Without US Leadership Amid Questions
headlines.summary: European allies downplayed concerns after US Defense Secretary Hegseth skipped the NATO gathering in Brussels, marking the second consecutive high-level US absence. Germany and Iceland insisted the alliance remains strong despite the unusual US no-shows.

headlines.headline: G7 Finance Chiefs Convene in Paris to Tackle Trade Imbalances
headlines.summary: Finance ministers from G7 nations meet in Paris to coordinate critical raw material supplies and address global economic tensions amid geopolitical differences threatening group cohesion.

headlines.headline: Israel to Establish Defense Offices in Former UNRWA East Jerusalem Compound
headlines.summary: Israel plans to set up defense offices in the former UNRWA compound in East Jerusalem after demolishing structures, a move the UN agency called a violation of international law.

perspectives.angle: Israel to Build Defense Offices in Former UNRWA Jerusalem Compound
perspectives.summary: Israel cabinet approved building a defense compound on the site of recently demolished UNRWA premises in East Jerusalem. The UN had called the demolition a violation of international law.

headlines.headline: UAE Pivots from Oil to AI as Post-War Economic Strategy
headlines.summary: With its OPEC era over, the UAE is betting big on artificial intelligence to build economic bridges to the Global South, bolstered by cutting-edge Nvidia chip deliveries. The strategy has been tested by Iranian attacks on Emirati data centers during the US-Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: Kremlin Signals Readiness to Resume Dialogue with Europe on Ukraine War
headlines.summary: Kremlin spokesperson Dmitry Peskov said Moscow is open to restarting dialogue with Europe over the war in Ukraine, following a major Ukrainian drone attack on the Moscow region. The comments come as European officials debate future engagement with Moscow amid uncertainty over US peace efforts.

headlines.headline: German Finance Minister Calls G7 the Right Forum to End Iran Conflict
headlines.summary: German Finance Minister Lars Klingbeil says the G7 is the appropriate platform to discuss ending the war in Iran, which along with the Strait of Hormuz blockade poses a serious threat to the global economy. The statement comes ahead of the G7 finance ministers meeting in Paris.

headlines.headline: CDC Aiding Withdrawal of Americans Potentially Exposed to Ebola in Congo
headlines.summary: The US Centers for Disease Control and Prevention is helping withdraw Americans who may have been exposed to Ebola during the outbreak in Congo and Uganda. A CDC official stressed that the risk to the United States remains low.

headlines.headline: Washington Post: CDC Readies Team to Respond to Ebola Outbreak
headlines.summary: The CDC is preparing a response team as the WHO declares the Ebola outbreak in Congo and Uganda a public health emergency. Health officials are coordinating with international partners to contain the spread of the virus.

headlines.headline: Taiwan President responds to Trump summit: 'We will not be sacrificed'
headlines.summary: Taiwan President Lai Ching-te pushed back after Trump raised doubts about US arms sales and willingness to defend the island following his Beijing summit with Xi Jinping.

perspectives.angle: Taiwan president defends US arms purchases after Trump calls them a bargaining chip
perspectives.summary: Taiwan's leader pushed back against criticism from former President Trump, defending the island's military purchases from the US as essential for deterrence against China.

headlines.headline: Senate parliamentarian blocks $1 billion White House security funding
headlines.summary: The Senate parliamentarian ruled that Republican efforts to allocate funding for Trump's proposed White House ballroom project cannot proceed through budget reconciliation.

perspectives.angle: Senate Parliamentarian Blocks $1 Billion White House Security Proposal
perspectives.summary: The Senate parliamentarian ruled that a $1 billion proposal for White House security funding, including a proposed ballroom, cannot proceed under budget reconciliation rules. The decision deals a blow to administration spending plans.

headlines.headline: Political executions surge in Iran since war began, UN verifies at least 32
headlines.summary: The United Nations has verified the execution of at least 32 political prisoners in Iran since the US-Israel war began on February 28.

headlines.headline: IDF Extends Death Penalty Law to West Bank Palestinians Convicted of Terror
headlines.summary: Israel Defense Forces Central Command chief has applied a new death penalty law to Palestinians in the West Bank convicted of terrorism, expanding capital punishment beyond Israel proper.

headlines.headline: Argentina Retires A-4 Fightinghawks After Six Decades
headlines.summary: Argentina has finally stood down its A-4 Skyhawks, the type's first export operator, after 60 years of service as the country introduces its first F-16s.

headlines.headline: Norway Arrests Chinese Man for Spying
headlines.summary: Norway's interior security service has arrested a Chinese national on suspicion of espionage, marking another case of alleged Chinese intelligence activity in Europe.

headlines.headline: Cuba Accuses US of Fabricating Drone Threat Claims
headlines.summary: Cuban Foreign Minister Bruno Rodriguez dismissed US intelligence reports about Cuban drone acquisitions from Russia and Iran as a "fraudulent case" designed to justify sanctions and potential military intervention.

headlines.headline: Moldova Condemns Russian Offer of Citizenship to Transdniestria Separatists
headlines.summary: Moldovan leaders denounced President Putin's offer of simplified Russian citizenship for the pro-Russian Transdniestria region as a threat to sovereignty, amid escalating tensions over the separatist enclave.

perspectives.angle: Moldova Condemns Russian Offer of Citizenship to Separatist Region
perspectives.summary: Moldovan leaders denounced Russian President Vladimir Putin's offer of simplified Russian citizenship for the pro-Russian Transdniestria separatist enclave as a threat, contemplating measures to counteract it.

headlines.headline: Iran Excludes Star Forward Azmoun from Preliminary World Cup Squad
headlines.summary: Iran's national team left out attacking talisman Sardar Azmoun from its preliminary World Cup squad as the team prepares for the tournament amid ongoing diplomatic tensions over participation.

headlines.headline: Air Show Collision Under Investigation at Idaho Air Force Base
headlines.summary: Mountain Home Air Force Base in Idaho is investigating an aircraft collision during an air show performance, with no immediate reports of casualties.

headlines.headline: Bond Yield Spike Poses Risk to Unprepared Equities Market
headlines.summary: Rising benchmark yields threaten equity valuations as companies and consumers face higher borrowing costs, with investors warning that markets may be underestimating the impact on growth and corporate profits.

headlines.headline: Trump warns 'clock is ticking' for Iran as peace negotiations stall
headlines.summary: President Trump issued a stark warning to Iran that 'the clock is ticking' as ceasefire negotiations remain deadlocked. The US and Iran remain at impasse over ending the war, with neither side agreeing to terms for a permanent peace deal.

perspectives.angle: Trump Warns Iran "Clock Is Ticking" as Peace Talks Stall
perspectives.summary: President Trump issued a stern warning to Iran as peace negotiations show signs of stalling, with Iranian media reporting the US has not made concrete concessions in response to Tehran's latest proposals.

headlines.headline: Netanyahu faces potential political downfall as Israeli government teeters
headlines.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Binyamin Netanyahu may be facing his final days in power as political pressures mount. The Economist analyzes whether the long-serving leader can survive the current crisis.

headlines.headline: Gulf states push to restore maritime navigation as Iran faces trust deficit
headlines.summary: A Gulf official told Al Arabiya that Iran must work to restore trust with GCC states. Regional powers are stepping up efforts to secure freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz amid ongoing tensions.

perspectives.angle: Gulf States Edge Closer to Direct Confrontation with Iran
perspectives.summary: Saudi Arabia and UAE have conducted military strikes inside Iran in retaliation for Iranian attacks, marking a shift from proxy conflicts to direct engagement. Kuwait also arrested IRGC members plotting attacks on Bubiyan Island.

headlines.headline: Air Force Base Locked Down After Idaho Air Show Incident
headlines.summary: Emergency crews responded to an aircraft incident during the Gunfighter Skies Air Show at Mountain Home Air Force Base in Idaho. Witnesses reported mid-air collision involving Navy jets with parachutes seen descending.

perspectives.angle: US Navy Growler Jets Collide During Idaho Air Show
perspectives.summary: Two EA-18G Growler electronic warfare jets collided during an air show in Idaho, with all four crew members ejecting safely as the aircraft plummeted to the ground.

headlines.headline: Republicans Vie to Replace Defeated Senator Cassidy in Louisiana
headlines.summary: Republican Senator Bill Cassidy was defeated in the primary after his impeachment vote against Trump. Representative Julia Letlow and State Treasurer John Fleming advance to the runoff.

headlines.headline: Cuba seeking Russian drones, military equipment as US warns of Guantanamo threat
headlines.summary: US intelligence reports Cuba has acquired over 300 Russian and Iranian drones and is discussing attacks on Guantanamo Bay, Key West, and US military vessels. Cuban officials are studying how Iran has resisted US military pressure.

headlines.headline: New Epstein victims emerge in French investigation
headlines.summary: Paris prosecutor says around 10 new victims have come forward since France launched a special probe into Jeffrey Epstein's network in February. Investigators are working to interview victims located abroad.

headlines.headline: Trump to Hold Situation Room Meeting Tuesday on Iran Military Options
headlines.summary: President Trump is expected to meet with top national security advisers on Tuesday to discuss military options regarding Iran, as ceasefire negotiations remain stalled.

perspectives.angle: Trump Called Off Planned Tuesday Attack on Iran, Citing Pending Deal
perspectives.summary: President Trump announced he postponed planned attacks on Iran at the request of Gulf allies. Iran said it is not intimidated by threats but talks continue to end the conflict.

headlines.headline: Israel Economy Contracts in Q1 as Iran War Takes Toll
headlines.summary: Israels economy shrank at an annualized 3.3% in the first quarter, hit by the war with Iran, but growth is expected to recover if the conflict does not reignite.

perspectives.angle: Report Reveals Israel Ran Second Covert Military Base in Iraq During Iran War
perspectives.summary: A previously undisclosed Israeli military outpost in western Iraq came to light after a Bedouin shepherd was killed near the site. The bases were used for air support, refueling and medical evacuation operations during the 12-day war with Iran.

headlines.headline: US Intelligence Warns of Cuban Drone Threat to Guantanamo Bay
headlines.summary: Classified intelligence reports Cuba has acquired over 300 military drones and discussed plans to attack the US base at Guantanamo Bay, US naval vessels, and potentially Key West, Florida.

headlines.headline: Kremlin Signals Interest in Restoring Dialogue With Europe
headlines.summary: Russian spokesperson Dmitry Peskov said Moscow could restart dialogue with Europe over the war in Ukraine, speaking after the large-scale Ukrainian drone attack on Moscow. The remarks come as European officials debate future engagement with Russia.

headlines.headline: Train crash kills 8 in Bangkok, driver charged with negligence
headlines.summary: A freight train collided with a public bus at a rail crossing in central Bangkok, killing eight people and injuring 32. Thai police have charged the train driver with negligence as investigations continue.

headlines.headline: Islamist attack on Nigerian military school kills 17 police trainees
headlines.summary: Suspected Islamist militants attacked a specialized military school in Yobe state, killing at least 17 police officers in training. The attack marks a major escalation in northeast Nigeria's ongoing insurgency.

headlines.headline: New York's largest commuter rail system shuts down in first strike since 1994
headlines.summary: The Long Island Rail Road ceased operations as five unions walked off the job after months of stalled contract negotiations. Governor Hochul pleaded with unions to resume talks before Monday's rush hour, as 250,000 daily commuters face disruption.

perspectives.angle: New York's Long Island Rail Road shut down by union strike
perspectives.summary: North America's largest commuter rail system halted operations Saturday as unions launched a strike after stalled contract negotiations, disrupting travel for thousands of commuters.

headlines.headline: Peru electoral body pledges to fix voting flaws before June presidential runoff
headlines.summary: Peru's top electoral authority acknowledged flaws that delayed April's first-round results by a month. The runoff between Keiko Fujimori and Roberto Sánchez is scheduled for June 7, following weeks of political uncertainty.

perspectives.angle: Peru Confirms June 7 Presidential Runoff Between Fujimori and Sánchez
perspectives.summary: Peruvian electoral authorities officially confirmed the presidential runoff election scheduled for June 7, setting up a contest between Keiko Fujimori and Pedro Sánchez.

headlines.headline: USS Ford Aircraft Carrier Returns Home After 11-Month Iran War Deployment
headlines.summary: The USS Ford aircraft carrier has returned to its home port after an 11-month deployment that supported operations related to the Iran conflict and the capture of Venezuelan leader Maduro. The carrier was one of the longest deployments for a US aircraft carrier in recent years.

perspectives.angle: USS Gerald R. Ford Returns After Record 11-Month Deployment Supporting Iran War and Venezuela Operation
perspectives.summary: The worlds largest aircraft carrier returned to Virginia after 326 days at sea, the longest deployment since Vietnam War, having supported combat operations against Iran and the capture of Venezuelas Maduro. The crew received the Presidential Unit Citation for outstanding performance in combat.

headlines.headline: Car Rams Into Pedestrians in Italy, Injuring Eight People
headlines.summary: A driver plowed into pedestrians in Italy, injuring eight people, four seriously. Passers-by chased and stopped the attacker. The incident is being investigated for possible terrorist motives.

headlines.headline: More Than 50 Schoolchildren Including Toddlers Kidnapped in Nigeria
headlines.summary: Armed gunmen have kidnapped more than 50 schoolchildren, including toddlers, in an attack on a school in Borno state, Nigeria. No group has claimed responsibility for the attack, which has devastated the local community.

perspectives.angle: Over 50 Schoolchildren Including Toddlers Kidnapped in Nigeria
perspectives.summary: Gunmen abducted more than 50 children, many aged 2-5 years old, from three schools in Mussa, Borno state. Attackers used children as human shields while fleeing on motorbikes. No group has claimed responsibility, though the attack bears hallmarks of Boko Haram activity in the region.

headlines.headline: US Navy plans Trump-class nuclear battleships to counter China
headlines.summary: The US Navy announced plans to build up to 15 nuclear-powered Trump-class battleships by 2055, dubbed the "Golden Fleet," though analysts question their vulnerability to anti-ship missiles and whether the US has sufficient industrial capacity.

perspectives.angle: Nuclear-Powered Trump Class Battleships to Reverse Navy Decision
perspectives.summary: Chief of Naval Operations says abandoning nuclear-powered surface combatants was one of the worst decisions the service ever made, as the new Trump class battleships will feature nuclear propulsion.

headlines.headline: SBU strikes Moscow defense plants and Crimea airbase in coordinated drone raid
headlines.summary: Ukrainian SBU and armed forces hit the Angstrem semiconductor plant in Zelenograd, the Moscow oil refinery, and military airfields in Crimea in one of the largest drone operations against Russian military-industrial targets.

headlines.headline: US to indict Raúl Castro over 1996 Brothers to the Rescue shootdown
headlines.summary: The US Justice Department will formally charge former Cuban president Raúl Castro next Wednesday in Miami for the 1996 downing of two civilian aircraft operated by the exile group Brothers to the Rescue, which killed four volunteers.

headlines.headline: Iranian president thanks neighbors for blocking territory use against Iran
headlines.summary: Iran President Masoud Pezeshkian praised neighboring countries for preventing "misuse of their territory" against Iran during a meeting with Pakistan interior minister, amid ongoing regional tensions and ceasefire negotiations.

headlines.headline: AIPAC leads record spending to unseat Thomas Massie in House primary
headlines.summary: Pro-Israel groups are pouring unprecedented funds into defeating Rep. Thomas Massie (R-KY) in what has become the most expensive House primary in history, with AIPAC and aligned super PACs spending millions on attack ads.

perspectives.angle: GOP Rep. Massie Introduces AIPAC Act Targeting Foreign Lobbying Days Before Primary
perspectives.summary: Rep. Thomas Massie, the harshest Republican critic of Israel in Congress, introduced legislation aimed at foreign lobbying efforts just days before his Kentucky primary. The bill targets AIPAC and similar organizations.

headlines.headline: US to Weigh Action Against China Over Industrial Overcapacity
headlines.summary: US Trade Ambassador says President Trump will be presented with options if investigations determine Chinese industrial overcapacity harms American interests, as trade tensions persist.

headlines.headline: Indonesia Pushes Back Against Chinese Business Complaints Over New Rules
headlines.summary: Chinese businesses issued a blunt warning to President Prabowo that tougher regulations are hurting investor confidence, revealing tensions between Jakarta push for resource control and foreign capital.

headlines.headline: White House to Host Mass Prayer Event Promoting Christian Origins of US
